Class DefaultNodeBehavior

java.lang.Object
dom.institution.lab.cns.bitcoin.node.DefaultNodeBehavior
All Implemented Interfaces:
NodeBehaviorStrategy
Direct Known Subclasses:
HiddenChainAttackBehavior, HonestNodeBehavior, MaliciousNodeBehavior_Deprecated
public abstract class DefaultNodeBehavior extends Object implements NodeBehaviorStrategy
An abstract base class providing default behaviors for Bitcoin network nodes within the Simulation environment.

This class encapsulates common node behaviors such as transaction receipt, mining initiation and termination, and mining pool reconstruction. Concrete behavior strategies (e.g., honest, selfish, or experimental nodes) extend this class and implement event-handling methods corresponding to key simulation events and/or override the default implementations herein.

The DefaultNodeBehavior acts as a strategy implementation shared by instances of BitcoinNode. Its role is to decide when and how a node participates in mining activities depending on simulation parameters, transaction value thresholds, and block size constraints.

See Also:
  • BitcoinNode
  • NodeBehaviorStrategy
  • Block

  • Field Details

    • node

      protected BitcoinNode node
      The BitcoinNode to which this behavior strategy is attached. Provides access to node-level state such as mining status, transaction pool, and scheduling functions.

  • Constructor Details

    • DefaultNodeBehavior

      public DefaultNodeBehavior()

  • Method Details

    • considerMining

      protected void considerMining(long time)
      Evaluates whether the node should be mining and manages transitions between mining and idle states.

      This method ensures consistency between node state, mining status, and pending validation events. Depending on whether mining is deemed worthwhile (as determined by isWorthMining()), this method may:

      • Start mining by scheduling a validation event for a new block, or
      • Stop mining and invalidate any scheduled validation event.
      Parameters:
      time - the current simulation time
      See Also:
      • isWorthMining()
      • PoWNode.isMining()
      • PoWNode.startMining(long)
      • PoWNode.stopMining()

    • isWorthMining

      protected boolean isWorthMining()
      Determines whether the current mining pool is sufficiently valuable to justify mining activity.
      Returns:
      true if the total value of the node’s mining pool exceeds BitcoinNode.getMinValueToMine(), otherwise false

    • conflictFree

      protected boolean conflictFree(dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.transaction.Transaction t)
      Returns whether the transaction is free of conflicts with the node's current pool, mining pool, and blockchain structure.

      Looks up the transaction's conflict partner in the conflict registry and checks whether that partner is present anywhere in the node's state.

      Parameters:
      t - the transaction to check
      Returns:
      true if no conflicting transaction is present; false otherwise
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the conflict entry for t is uninitialized

    • transactionContainedInPool

      protected boolean transactionContainedInPool(dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.transaction.Transaction t)

    • transactionContainedInStructure

      protected boolean transactionContainedInStructure(dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.transaction.Transaction t)

    • dependenciesPresent

      protected boolean dependenciesPresent(dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.transaction.Transaction t)
      Returns whether all dependencies of the transaction are satisfied.

      Delegates to TransactionGroup#satisfiesDependenciesOf_Incl_3rdGroup, which checks the pool, mining pool, and the full blockchain structure as a third group.

      Parameters:
      t - the transaction to check
      Returns:
      true if all dependencies are satisfied; false otherwise

    • getConflictBlock

      protected Block getConflictBlock(Block b)
      Builds a synthetic block containing the conflict-group counterparts of each transaction in b, based on the node's conflict registry.

      The returned transactions are manufactured stand-ins (same ID only); they are not the real transaction objects. Transaction equality is assumed to be determined by ID.

      Parameters:
      b - the block whose transactions are examined for conflicts
      Returns:
      a new Block containing one stand-in transaction per conflicting transaction in b; empty if none conflict

    • handleNewBlockReception

      protected void handleNewBlockReception(Block b)
      Integrates a newly received block into the node's blockchain and updates the transaction pool accordingly.

      Adds the block to the structure, removes its transactions from the pool, reconstructs the mining pool, and reconsiders whether to mine.

      Parameters:
      b - the newly received and validated block

    • processPostValidationActivities

      protected void processPostValidationActivities(long time)
      Performs cleanup and re-initialization steps following successful block validation.

      Stops mining, resets the pending validation event, removes the validated transactions from the mining pool, reconstructs the pool, and reconsiders whether to restart mining.

      Parameters:
      time - the current simulation time

    • reconstructMiningPool

      protected void reconstructMiningPool()
      Reconstructs the node’s mining pool by selecting the top transactions according to value-per-size ratio.

      The number of transactions included is limited by the configuration property bitcoin.maxBlockSize.

      See Also:
      • TxValuePerSizeComparator

    • transactionReceipt

      protected void transactionReceipt(dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.transaction.Transaction t, long time)
      Handles the receipt of a transaction and updates the mining pool accordingly.

      This method adds the transaction to the pool, reconstructs the mining pool, and then re-evaluates whether mining should begin or continue. This default implementation does so by executing the following methods in this sequence:

      • Node.addTransactionToPool(Transaction)
      • reconstructMiningPool()
      • considerMining(long)
      Parameters:
      t - the received transaction
      time - the current simulation time

    • event_NodeReceivesClientTransaction

      public abstract void event_NodeReceivesClientTransaction(dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.transaction.Transaction t, long time)
      Invoked when the node receives a transaction directly from a client.
      Specified by:
      event_NodeReceivesClientTransaction in interface NodeBehaviorStrategy
      Parameters:
      t - the transaction received from the client
      time - the current simulation time

    • event_NodeReceivesPropagatedTransaction

      public abstract void event_NodeReceivesPropagatedTransaction(dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.transaction.Transaction t, long time)
      Invoked when the node receives a transaction propagated from another node.
      Specified by:
      event_NodeReceivesPropagatedTransaction in interface NodeBehaviorStrategy
      Parameters:
      t - the propagated transaction
      time - the current simulation time

    • event_NodeReceivesPropagatedContainer

      public abstract void event_NodeReceivesPropagatedContainer(dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.transaction.ITxContainer t)
      Invoked when the node receives a propagated container of transactions, such as a block or batch of transactions.
      Specified by:
      event_NodeReceivesPropagatedContainer in interface NodeBehaviorStrategy
      Parameters:
      t - the propagated transaction container

    • event_NodeCompletesValidation

      public abstract void event_NodeCompletesValidation(dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.transaction.ITxContainer t, long time)
      Invoked when the node completes validation of a transaction container, such as a newly mined or propagated block.
      Specified by:
      event_NodeCompletesValidation in interface NodeBehaviorStrategy
      Parameters:
      t - the validated transaction container
      time - the current simulation time
